subject: Public Health
title: Pit Latrines and Improved Sanitation
description: Learn Pit Latrines and Improved Sanitation in one minute.

hook.text: A well-designed pit latrine can reduce diarrheal disease by 36%—with no running water required.

buildup.text: Improved latrines separate waste from human contact—using slabs, ventilation pipes, and sealed pits.

discovery.text: The simple ventilated improved pit latrine was a game-changer for rural sanitation across Africa and Asia.

twist.text: Latrine pits eventually fill up. Without desludging services, families abandon them and return to open defecation.

climax.text: The full sanitation chain—from toilet to treatment—must work, or gains are lost.

punchline.text: A latrine without maintenance is a broken promise to public health.

quiz.question: By how much can a well-designed pit latrine reduce diarrheal disease?
